sanskrit:
भोगैश्वर्यप्रसक्तानां तयापहृतचेतसाम्।
व्यवसायात्मिका बुद्धिः समाधौ न विधीयते॥
transliteration:
bhogaiśvarya-prasaktānāṃ tayāpahṛta-cetasām |
vyavasāyātmikā buddhiḥ samādhau na vidhīyate ||
literal_meaning: For those clinging to pleasure-and-power, whose minds are stolen by them, the resolute intellect that leads to samādhi is not established.
contextual_meaning: Krishna warns Arjuna that compulsive chasing of comfort, status and shiny objects hijacks the pre-frontal cortex; no clear, values-aligned decision (vyavasāya) can form while the reward circuitry is colonised by ‘more, better, now’.
psychological_state: Addicted to the dopamine drip of consumer culture, the mind oscillates between manic pursuit and numb crash, making long-range purposeful action impossible.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling luxury-home reels at 2 a.m., feeling the itch to leverage crypto for a down-payment, while the dream of writing that novel keeps receding.
anchor_text: Let go of pleasure and power to steady your mind.
